response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a strong background in web platforms, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but his profile has essentially no overlap with the core requirements of a Principal Performance Architect focused on AI accelerator hardware. There is no evidence of SoC or IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, deep-learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design for AI systems. His closest hardware-adjacent work — robotic arm firmware and a wave field synthesis thesis — are too peripheral and dated to constitute relevant experience at the principal level this role demands.
strengths: Demonstrated low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, high-performance databases at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests/second) shows some performance engineering instinct
Multi-language proficiency including C/C++ and DLang indicates comfort operating closer to the metal than a typical web developer
Long tenure as a Staff/Senior IC with consistent technical leadership, open-source contributions, and conference talks signals strong engineering depth and communication ability
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, which is a non-negotiable core requirement of the role
No background in AI/ML hardware, GPU architectures, deep-neural-network workload profiling, or any accelerator-adjacent domain

Educational background (BS in CS + BS in Photography/Design) does not meet the preferred depth for a principal-level hardware architecture role, and total experience is entirely in software, not hardware or system architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU architecture background
